notes: 15-clue draft for music_performing_arts. Covers Classical Composers (7), Opera (1), Broadway & Musicals (4), Theater (2), Film Scores & Soundtracks (1). Repeated answers (Beethoven's Fifth, 1812 Overture, Mahler, Oh Mary!, Hedwig) are intentional — each pair targets a different difficulty level.

questions.clue: This frequently performed work for orchestra opens with a distinctive 4-note 'short-short-short-long' motif, often characterized as 'fate knocking at the door'; it was first performed in Vienna in 1808.

citations.title: Symphony No. 5 (Beethoven)
citations.snippet: The Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67, is a symphony composed by Ludwig van Beethoven between 1804 and 1808. It is one of the best-known of all symphonies and one of the most frequently played. First performed in Vienna in 1808, the work ac

questions.clue: This Tchaikovsky concert piece, written in 1880, commemorates Russia's successful defense against a French invasion; it was first performed in Moscow on August 20, 1882, under a tent near the still-unfinished Cathedral of Christ the Saviour.

citations.title: 1812 Overture
citations.snippet: The Year 1812, Solemn Overture, Op. 49, popularly known as the 1812 Overture, is a concert overture in E♭ major written in 1880 by Russian composer Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky. The piece commemorates Russia's successful defence against the Fre

questions.clue: This 1928 work for large orchestra by French composer Maurice Ravel is one of his most famous compositions and was among his last completed works before illness diminished his ability to compose.

citations.title: Boléro
citations.snippet: Boléro is a 1928 work for large orchestra by French composer Maurice Ravel. It is one of Ravel's most famous compositions. It was also one of his last completed works before illness diminished his ability to write music.

questions.clue: This Tchaikovsky piece is best known for its climactic volley of cannon fire, ringing chimes, and brass fanfare finale; it has since become a popular accompaniment to United States Independence Day fireworks displays.

citations.title: 1812 Overture

questions.clue: This Austro-Bohemian Romantic composer, whose music gained wide popularity only after periods of neglect including a Nazi-era ban, is regarded as a bridge between 19th-century tradition and the modernism of the early 20th century.

citations.title: Gustav Mahler

questions.clue: E. T. A. Hoffmann described this work as 'one of the most important works of the time' shortly after its 1808 Vienna premiere; it has since become one of the most frequently performed pieces in the classical repertoire.

questions.clue: This composer converted to Catholicism in 1897 to secure his appointment as director of Vienna's Court Opera, where he faced anti-Semitic opposition despite his reputation for brilliant conducting; late in life he also led New York's Metropolitan Opera and the Philharmonic.

questions.clue: This Bavarian town, whose roots date to 1194, hosts an annual festival devoted entirely to the operas of German Romantic composer Richard Wagner.

citations.title: Bayreuth
citations.snippet: Bayreuth is a town in northern Bavaria, Germany, on the Red Main river in a valley between the Franconian Jura and the Fichtel Mountains. The town's roots date back to 1194. In the 21st century, it is the capital of Upper Franconia and has

questions.clue: This fictional Fleet Street barber, who first appeared as the villain in the 1846-1847 penny dreadful serial 'The String of Pearls,' murders his customers with a straight razor and enlists Mrs. Lovett to bake their remains into meat pies.

citations.title: Sweeney Todd
citations.snippet: Sweeney Todd is a fictional character who first appeared as the villain of the penny dreadful serial The String of Pearls (1846–1847). The original tale became a feature of 19th-century melodrama and London legend. A barber from Fleet Stree

questions.clue: This Ohio-born exhibition shooter starred in Buffalo Bill's Wild West alongside husband Frank Butler; in 1894 Thomas Edison filmed her acts for one of his earliest Kinetoscopes, and her story later inspired a celebrated Broadway musical.

citations.title: Annie Oakley
citations.snippet: Annie Oakley was an American exhibition/trick shooter and folk heroine who starred in Buffalo Bill's Wild West.

questions.clue: This 2024 Broadway comedy, written and originally performed by Cole Escola, transferred from off-Broadway at the Lucille Lortel to the Lyceum Theatre and was named a Pulitzer Prize for Drama finalist.

citations.title: Oh, Mary!
citations.snippet: Oh, Mary! is a comedy play written and originally performed by American comedian Cole Escola. The show opened on Broadway on July 11, 2024, at the Lyceum Theatre, transferring from its off-Broadway run at the Lucille Lortel Theatre, earning

questions.clue: Cole Escola's self-written Broadway vehicle made theatrical history when its star won the Tony Award for Best Actor in a Play as the first non-binary performer to receive that recognition.

questions.clue: This 2001 film was based on the 1998 stage musical by John Cameron Mitchell and Stephen Trask; it follows a gay East German rock singer who becomes a mentor and musical collaborator to a younger man who then steals her music.

citations.title: Hedwig and the Angry Inch (film)
citations.snippet: Hedwig and the Angry Inch is a 2001 American musical comedy-drama film written for the screen and directed by John Cameron Mitchell. Based on Mitchell's and Stephen Trask's 1998 stage musical Hedwig and the Angry Inch, it accompanies Hedwig

questions.clue: Despite its positive critical reception, this 2001 musical film grossed only $3.6 million against a $6 million budget; it won both the Best Director and Audience Awards at the Sundance Film Festival and has since developed a devoted cult following.

questions.clue: The score for this 1998 Coen Brothers film, composed by Carter Burwell, underscores the story of a Los Angeles slacker who is mistaken for a wealthy man of the same surname; Joel Coen described it as his 'Chandler kind of story.'

citations.title: The Big Lebowski
